verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचतत उत्पन्नविज्ञाना आश्वधोक्षजभाषितम् ।स्मरन्त आत्मजे भार्यां विसृज्य प्राव्रजन् गृहात् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācatata utpanna-vijñānāāśv adhokṣaja-bhāṣitamsmaranta ātmaje bhāryāṁvisṛjya prāvrajan gṛhāt
verses.text: The great saint Maitreya continued: After that, the Pracetās lived at home for thousands of years and developed perfect knowledge in spiritual consciousness. At last they remembered the blessings of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and left home, putting their wife in charge of a perfect son.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचतत उत्पन्नविज्ञाना आश्वधोक्षजभाषितम् ।स्मरन्त आत्मजे भार्यां विसृज्य प्राव्रजन् गृहात् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācatata utpanna-vijñānāāśv adhokṣaja-bhāṣitamsmaranta ātmaje bhāryāṁvisṛjya prāvrajan gṛhāt
verses.text: The great saint Maitreya continued: After that, the Pracetās lived at home for thousands of years and developed perfect knowledge in spiritual consciousness. At last they remembered the blessings of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and left home, putting their wife in charge of a perfect son.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचतत उत्पन्नविज्ञाना आश्वधोक्षजभाषितम् ।स्मरन्त आत्मजे भार्यां विसृज्य प्राव्रजन् गृहात् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācatata utpanna-vijñānāāśv adhokṣaja-bhāṣitamsmaranta ātmaje bhāryāṁvisṛjya prāvrajan gṛhāt
verses.text: The great saint Maitreya continued: After that, the Pracetās lived at home for thousands of years and developed perfect knowledge in spiritual consciousness. At last they remembered the blessings of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and left home, putting their wife in charge of a perfect son.

verses.sanskrit: दीक्षिता ब्रह्मसत्रेण सर्वभूतात्ममेधसा ।प्रतीच्यां दिशि वेलायां सिद्धोऽभूद्यत्र जाजलि: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: dīkṣitā brahma-satreṇasarva-bhūtātma-medhasāpratīcyāṁ diśi velāyāṁsiddho ’bhūd yatra jājaliḥ
verses.text: The Pracetās went to the seashore in the west where the great liberated sage Jājali was residing. After perfecting the spiritual knowledge by which one becomes equal toward all living entities, the Pracetās became perfect in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: दीक्षिता ब्रह्मसत्रेण सर्वभूतात्ममेधसा ।प्रतीच्यां दिशि वेलायां सिद्धोऽभूद्यत्र जाजलि: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: dīkṣitā brahma-satreṇasarva-bhūtātma-medhasāpratīcyāṁ diśi velāyāṁsiddho ’bhūd yatra jājaliḥ
verses.text: The Pracetās went to the seashore in the west where the great liberated sage Jājali was residing. After perfecting the spiritual knowledge by which one becomes equal toward all living entities, the Pracetās became perfect in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: दीक्षिता ब्रह्मसत्रेण सर्वभूतात्ममेधसा ।प्रतीच्यां दिशि वेलायां सिद्धोऽभूद्यत्र जाजलि: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: dīkṣitā brahma-satreṇasarva-bhūtātma-medhasāpratīcyāṁ diśi velāyāṁsiddho ’bhūd yatra jājaliḥ
verses.text: The Pracetās went to the seashore in the west where the great liberated sage Jājali was residing. After perfecting the spiritual knowledge by which one becomes equal toward all living entities, the Pracetās became perfect in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: दीक्षिता ब्रह्मसत्रेण सर्वभूतात्ममेधसा ।प्रतीच्यां दिशि वेलायां सिद्धोऽभूद्यत्र जाजलि: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: dīkṣitā brahma-satreṇasarva-bhūtātma-medhasāpratīcyāṁ diśi velāyāṁsiddho ’bhūd yatra jājaliḥ
verses.text: The Pracetās went to the seashore in the west where the great liberated sage Jājali was residing. After perfecting the spiritual knowledge by which one becomes equal toward all living entities, the Pracetās became perfect in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: दीक्षिता ब्रह्मसत्रेण सर्वभूतात्ममेधसा ।प्रतीच्यां दिशि वेलायां सिद्धोऽभूद्यत्र जाजलि: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: dīkṣitā brahma-satreṇasarva-bhūtātma-medhasāpratīcyāṁ diśi velāyāṁsiddho ’bhūd yatra jājaliḥ
verses.text: The Pracetās went to the seashore in the west where the great liberated sage Jājali was residing. After perfecting the spiritual knowledge by which one becomes equal toward all living entities, the Pracetās became perfect in Kṛṣṇa consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: तान्निर्जितप्राणमनोवचोद‍ृशोजितासनान् शान्तसमानविग्रहान् ।परेऽमले ब्रह्मणि योजितात्मन:सुरासुरेड्यो दद‍ृशे स्म नारद: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān nirjita-prāṇa-mano-vaco-dṛśojitāsanān śānta-samāna-vigrahānpare ’male brahmaṇi yojitātmanaḥsurāsureḍyo dadṛśe sma nāradaḥ
verses.text: After practicing the yogāsana for mystic yoga, the Pracetās managed to control their life air, mind, words and external vision. Thus by the prāṇāyāma process they were completely relieved of material attachment. By remaining perpendicular, they could concentrate their minds on the uppermost Brahman. While they were practicing this prāṇāyāma, the great sage Nārada, who is worshiped both by demons and by demigods, came to see them.

verses.sanskrit: तान्निर्जितप्राणमनोवचोद‍ृशोजितासनान् शान्तसमानविग्रहान् ।परेऽमले ब्रह्मणि योजितात्मन:सुरासुरेड्यो दद‍ृशे स्म नारद: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān nirjita-prāṇa-mano-vaco-dṛśojitāsanān śānta-samāna-vigrahānpare ’male brahmaṇi yojitātmanaḥsurāsureḍyo dadṛśe sma nāradaḥ
verses.text: After practicing the yogāsana for mystic yoga, the Pracetās managed to control their life air, mind, words and external vision. Thus by the prāṇāyāma process they were completely relieved of material attachment. By remaining perpendicular, they could concentrate their minds on the uppermost Brahman. While they were practicing this prāṇāyāma, the great sage Nārada, who is worshiped both by demons and by demigods, came to see them.

verses.sanskrit: तमागतं त उत्थाय प्रणिपत्याभिनन्द्य च ।पूजयित्वा यथादेशं सुखासीनमथाब्रुवन् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam āgataṁ ta utthāyapraṇipatyābhinandya capūjayitvā yathādeśaṁsukhāsīnam athābruvan
verses.text: As soon as the Pracetās saw that the great sage Nārada had appeared, they immediately got up even from their āsanas. As required, they immediately offered obeisances and worshiped him, and when they saw that Nārada Muni was properly seated, they began to ask him questions.

verses.sanskrit: प्रचेतस ऊचु:स्वागतं ते सुरर्षेऽद्य दिष्ट्या नो दर्शनं गत: ।तव चङ्‌क्रमणं ब्रह्मन्नभयाय यथा रवे: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: pracetasa ūcuḥsvāgataṁ te surarṣe ’dyadiṣṭyā no darśanaṁ gataḥtava caṅkramaṇaṁ brahmannabhayāya yathā raveḥ
verses.text: All the Pracetās began to address the great sage Nārada: O great sage, O brāhmaṇa, we hope you met with no disturbances while coming here. It is due to our great fortune that we are now able to see you. By the traveling of the sun, people are relieved from the fear of the darkness of night — a fear brought about by thieves and rogues. Similarly, your traveling is like the sun’s, for you drive away all kinds of fear.

verses.sanskrit: यदादिष्टं भगवता शिवेनाधोक्षजेन च ।तद् गृहेषु प्रसक्तानां प्रायश: क्षपितं प्रभो ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad ādiṣṭaṁ bhagavatāśivenādhokṣajena catad gṛheṣu prasaktānāṁprāyaśaḥ kṣapitaṁ prabho
verses.text: O master, may we inform you that because of our being overly attached to family affairs, we almost forgot the instructions we received from Lord Śiva and Lord Viṣṇu.

verses.sanskrit: तन्न: प्रद्योतयाध्यात्मज्ञानं तत्त्वार्थदर्शनम् ।येनाञ्जसा तरिष्यामो दुस्तरं भवसागरम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tan naḥ pradyotayādhyātma-jñānaṁ tattvārtha-darśanamyenāñjasā tariṣyāmodustaraṁ bhava-sāgaram
verses.text: Dear master, kindly enlighten us in transcendental knowledge, which may act as a torchlight by which we may cross the dark nescience of material existence.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचइति प्रचेतसां पृष्टो भगवान्नारदो मुनि: ।भगवत्युत्तमश्लोक आविष्टात्माब्रवीन्नृपान् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaiti pracetasāṁ pṛṣṭobhagavān nārado muniḥbhagavaty uttama-ślokaāviṣṭātmābravīn nṛpān
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: My dear Vidura, being thus petitioned by the Pracetās, the supreme devotee Nārada, who is always absorbed in thoughts of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, began to reply.

verses.sanskrit: नारद उवाचतज्जन्म तानि कर्माणि तदायुस्तन्मनो वच: ।नृणां येन हि विश्वात्मा सेव्यते हरिरीश्वर: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nārada uvācataj janma tāni karmāṇitad āyus tan mano vacaḥnṛṇāṁ yena hi viśvātmāsevyate harir īśvaraḥ
verses.text: The great sage Nārada said: When a living entity is born to engage in the devotional service of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the supreme controller, his birth, all his fruitive activities, his life span, his mind and his words are all factually perfect.

verses.sanskrit: नारद उवाचतज्जन्म तानि कर्माणि तदायुस्तन्मनो वच: ।नृणां येन हि विश्वात्मा सेव्यते हरिरीश्वर: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: nārada uvācataj janma tāni karmāṇitad āyus tan mano vacaḥnṛṇāṁ yena hi viśvātmāsevyate harir īśvaraḥ
verses.text: The great sage Nārada said: When a living entity is born to engage in the devotional service of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is the supreme controller, his birth, all his fruitive activities, his life span, his mind and his words are all factually perfect.

verses.sanskrit: किं जन्मभिस्त्रिभिर्वेह शौक्रसावित्रयाज्ञिकै: ।कर्मभिर्वा त्रयीप्रोक्तै: पुंसोऽपि विबुधायुषा ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ janmabhis tribhir vehaśaukra-sāvitra-yājñikaiḥkarmabhir vā trayī-proktaiḥpuṁso ’pi vibudhāyuṣā
verses.text: A civilized human being has three kinds of births. The first birth is by a pure father and mother, and this birth is called birth by semen. The next birth takes place when one is initiated by the spiritual master, and this birth is called sāvitra. The third birth, called yājñika, takes place when one is given the opportunity to worship Lord Viṣṇu. Despite the opportunities for attaining such births, even if one gets the life span of a demigod, if one does not actually engage in the service of the Lord, everything is useless. Similarly, one’s activities may be mundane or spiritual, but they are useless if they are not meant for satisfying the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: किं जन्मभिस्त्रिभिर्वेह शौक्रसावित्रयाज्ञिकै: ।कर्मभिर्वा त्रयीप्रोक्तै: पुंसोऽपि विबुधायुषा ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ janmabhis tribhir vehaśaukra-sāvitra-yājñikaiḥkarmabhir vā trayī-proktaiḥpuṁso ’pi vibudhāyuṣā
verses.text: A civilized human being has three kinds of births. The first birth is by a pure father and mother, and this birth is called birth by semen. The next birth takes place when one is initiated by the spiritual master, and this birth is called sāvitra. The third birth, called yājñika, takes place when one is given the opportunity to worship Lord Viṣṇu. Despite the opportunities for attaining such births, even if one gets the life span of a demigod, if one does not actually engage in the service of the Lord, everything is useless. Similarly, one’s activities may be mundane or spiritual, but they are useless if they are not meant for satisfying the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: किं जन्मभिस्त्रिभिर्वेह शौक्रसावित्रयाज्ञिकै: ।कर्मभिर्वा त्रयीप्रोक्तै: पुंसोऽपि विबुधायुषा ॥ १० ॥

The next birth takes place when one is initiated by the spiritual master, and this birth is called sāvitra. The third birth, called yājñika, takes place when one is given the opportunity to worship Lord Viṣṇu. Similarly, one’s activities may be mundane or spiritual, but they are useless if they are not meant for satisfying the Lord.

verses.text: A civilized human being has three kinds of births. The first birth is by a pure father and mother, and this birth is called birth by semen. The next birth takes place when one is initiated by the spiritual master, and this birth is called sāvitra. The third birth, called yājñika, takes place when one is given the opportunity to worship Lord Viṣṇu. Despite the opportunities for attaining such births, even if one gets the life span of a demigod, if one does not actually engage in the service of the Lord, everything is useless. Similarly, one’s activities may be mundane or spiritual, but they are useless if they are not meant for satisfying the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतेन तपसा वा किं वचोभिश्चित्तवृत्तिभि: ।बुद्ध्या वा किं निपुणया बलेनेन्द्रियराधसा ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutena tapasā vā kiṁvacobhiś citta-vṛttibhiḥbuddhyā vā kiṁ nipuṇayābalenendriya-rādhasā
verses.text: Without devotional service, what is the meaning of severe austerities, the process of hearing, the power of speech, the power of mental speculation, elevated intelligence, strength, and the power of the senses?

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतेन तपसा वा किं वचोभिश्चित्तवृत्तिभि: ।बुद्ध्या वा किं निपुणया बलेनेन्द्रियराधसा ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutena tapasā vā kiṁvacobhiś citta-vṛttibhiḥbuddhyā vā kiṁ nipuṇayābalenendriya-rādhasā
verses.text: Without devotional service, what is the meaning of severe austerities, the process of hearing, the power of speech, the power of mental speculation, elevated intelligence, strength, and the power of the senses?

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतेन तपसा वा किं वचोभिश्चित्तवृत्तिभि: ।बुद्ध्या वा किं निपुणया बलेनेन्द्रियराधसा ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutena tapasā vā kiṁvacobhiś citta-vṛttibhiḥbuddhyā vā kiṁ nipuṇayābalenendriya-rādhasā
verses.text: Without devotional service, what is the meaning of severe austerities, the process of hearing, the power of speech, the power of mental speculation, elevated intelligence, strength, and the power of the senses?

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतेन तपसा वा किं वचोभिश्चित्तवृत्तिभि: ।बुद्ध्या वा किं निपुणया बलेनेन्द्रियराधसा ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutena tapasā vā kiṁvacobhiś citta-vṛttibhiḥbuddhyā vā kiṁ nipuṇayābalenendriya-rādhasā
verses.text: Without devotional service, what is the meaning of severe austerities, the process of hearing, the power of speech, the power of mental speculation, elevated intelligence, strength, and the power of the senses?

verses.sanskrit: किं वा योगेन साङ्ख्येन न्यासस्वाध्याययोरपि ।किं वा श्रेयोभिरन्यैश्च न यत्रात्मप्रदो हरि: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ vā yogena sāṅkhyenanyāsa-svādhyāyayor apikiṁ vā śreyobhir anyaiś cana yatrātma-prado hariḥ
verses.text: Transcendental practices that do not ultimately help one realize the Supreme Personality of Godhead are useless, be they mystic yoga practices, the analytical study of matter, severe austerity, the acceptance of sannyāsa, or the study of Vedic literature. All these may be very important aspects of spiritual advancement, but unless one understands the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, all these processes are useless.

verses.sanskrit: किं वा योगेन साङ्ख्येन न्यासस्वाध्याययोरपि ।किं वा श्रेयोभिरन्यैश्च न यत्रात्मप्रदो हरि: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ vā yogena sāṅkhyenanyāsa-svādhyāyayor apikiṁ vā śreyobhir anyaiś cana yatrātma-prado hariḥ
verses.text: Transcendental practices that do not ultimately help one realize the Supreme Personality of Godhead are useless, be they mystic yoga practices, the analytical study of matter, severe austerity, the acceptance of sannyāsa, or the study of Vedic literature. All these may be very important aspects of spiritual advancement, but unless one understands the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, all these processes are useless.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयसामपि सर्वेषामात्मा ह्यवधिरर्थत: ।सर्वेषामपि भूतानां हरिरात्मात्मद: प्रिय: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śreyasām api sarveṣāmātmā hy avadhir arthataḥsarveṣām api bhūtānāṁharir ātmātmadaḥ priyaḥ
verses.text: Factually the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the original source of all self-realization. Consequently, the goal of all auspicious activities — karma, jñāna, yoga and bhakti — is the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयसामपि सर्वेषामात्मा ह्यवधिरर्थत: ।सर्वेषामपि भूतानां हरिरात्मात्मद: प्रिय: ॥ १३ ॥

verses.text: Factually the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the original source of all self-realization. Consequently, the goal of all auspicious activities — karma, jñāna, yoga and bhakti — is the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयसामपि सर्वेषामात्मा ह्यवधिरर्थत: ।सर्वेषामपि भूतानां हरिरात्मात्मद: प्रिय: ॥ १३ ॥

verses.sanskrit: यथा तरोर्मूलनिषेचनेनतृप्यन्ति तत्स्कन्धभुजोपशाखा: ।प्राणोपहाराच्च यथेन्द्रियाणांतथैव सर्वार्हणमच्युतेज्या ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā taror mūla-niṣecanenatṛpyanti tat-skandha-bhujopaśākhāḥprāṇopahārāc ca yathendriyāṇāṁtathaiva sarvārhaṇam acyutejyā
verses.text: As pouring water on the root of a tree energizes the trunk, branches, twigs and everything else, and as supplying food to the stomach enlivens the senses and limbs of the body, simply worshiping the Supreme Personality of Godhead through devotional service automatically satisfies the demigods, who are parts of that Supreme Personality.

verses.sanskrit: यथैव सूर्यात्प्रभवन्ति वार:पुनश्च तस्मिन्प्रविशन्ति काले ।भूतानि भूमौ स्थिरजङ्गमानितथा हरावेव गुणप्रवाह: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathaiva sūryāt prabhavanti vāraḥpunaś ca tasmin praviśanti kālebhūtāni bhūmau sthira-jaṅgamānitathā harāv eva guṇa-pravāhaḥ
verses.text: During the rainy season, water is generated from the sun, and in due course of time, during the summer season, the very same water is again absorbed by the sun. Similarly, all living entities, moving and inert, are generated from the earth, and again, after some time, they all return to the earth as dust. Similarly, everything emanates from the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and in due course of time everything enters into Him again.

verses.sanskrit: यथैव सूर्यात्प्रभवन्ति वार:पुनश्च तस्मिन्प्रविशन्ति काले ।भूतानि भूमौ स्थिरजङ्गमानितथा हरावेव गुणप्रवाह: ॥ १५ ॥

verses.text: During the rainy season, water is generated from the sun, and in due course of time, during the summer season, the very same water is again absorbed by the sun. Similarly, all living entities, moving and inert, are generated from the earth, and again, after some time, they all return to the earth as dust. Similarly, everything emanates from the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and in due course of time everything enters into Him again.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्पदं तज्जगदात्मन: परंसकृद्विभातं सवितुर्यथा प्रभा ।यथासवो जाग्रति सुप्तशक्तयोद्रव्यक्रियाज्ञानभिदाभ्रमात्यय: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: etat padaṁ taj jagad-ātmanaḥ paraṁsakṛd vibhātaṁ savitur yathā prabhāyathāsavo jāgrati supta-śaktayodravya-kriyā-jñāna-bhidā-bhramātyayaḥ
verses.text: Just as the sunshine is nondifferent from the sun, the cosmic manifestation is also nondifferent from the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The Supreme Personality is therefore all-pervasive within this material creation. When the senses are active, they appear to be part and parcel of the body, but when the body is asleep, their activities are unmanifest. Similarly, the whole cosmic creation appears different and yet nondifferent from the Supreme Person.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्पदं तज्जगदात्मन: परंसकृद्विभातं सवितुर्यथा प्रभा ।यथासवो जाग्रति सुप्तशक्तयोद्रव्यक्रियाज्ञानभिदाभ्रमात्यय: ॥ १६ ॥

When the senses are active, they appear to be part and parcel of the body, but when the body is asleep, their activities are unmanifest. verses.sanskrit: यथा नभस्यभ्रतम:प्रकाशाभवन्ति भूपा न भवन्त्यनुक्रमात् ।एवं परे ब्रह्मणि शक्तयस्त्वमूरजस्तम:सत्त्वमिति प्रवाह: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā nabhasy abhra-tamaḥ-prakāśābhavanti bhūpā na bhavanty anukramātevaṁ pare brahmaṇi śaktayas tv amūrajas tamaḥ sattvam iti pravāhaḥ
verses.text: My dear Kings, sometimes in the sky there are clouds, sometimes there is darkness, and sometimes there is illumination. The appearance of all these takes place consecutively. Similarly, in the Supreme Absolute, the modes of passion, darkness and goodness appear as consecutive energies. Sometimes they appear, and sometimes they disappear.

verses.sanskrit: यथा नभस्यभ्रतम:प्रकाशाभवन्ति भूपा न भवन्त्यनुक्रमात् ।एवं परे ब्रह्मणि शक्तयस्त्वमूरजस्तम:सत्त्वमिति प्रवाह: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā nabhasy abhra-tamaḥ-prakāśābhavanti bhūpā na bhavanty anukramātevaṁ pare brahmaṇi śaktayas tv amūrajas tamaḥ sattvam iti pravāhaḥ
verses.text: My dear Kings, sometimes in the sky there are clouds, sometimes there is darkness, and sometimes there is illumination. The appearance of all these takes place consecutively. Similarly, in the Supreme Absolute, the modes of passion, darkness and goodness appear as consecutive energies. Sometimes they appear, and sometimes they disappear.

verses.sanskrit: तेनैकमात्मानमशेषदेहिनांकालं प्रधानं पुरुषं परेशम् ।स्वतेजसा ध्वस्तगुणप्रवाह-मात्मैकभावेन भजध्वमद्धा ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tenaikam ātmānam aśeṣa-dehināṁkālaṁ pradhānaṁ puruṣaṁ pareśamsva-tejasā dhvasta-guṇa-pravāhamātmaika-bhāvena bhajadhvam addhā
verses.text: Because the Supreme Lord is the cause of all causes, He is the Supersoul of all individual living entities, and He exists as both the remote and immediate cause. Since He is aloof from the material emanations, He is free from their interactions and is Lord of material nature. You should therefore engage in His devotional service, thinking yourself qualitatively one with Him.

verses.sanskrit: दयया सर्वभूतेषु सन्तुष्ट्या येन केन वा ।सर्वेन्द्रियोपशान्त्या च तुष्यत्याशु जनार्दन: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dayayā sarva-bhūteṣusantuṣṭyā yena kena vāsarvendriyopaśāntyā catuṣyaty āśu janārdanaḥ
verses.text: By showing mercy to all living entities, being satisfied somehow or other, and restricting the senses from sense enjoyment, one can very quickly satisfy the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Janārdana.

verses.sanskrit: दयया सर्वभूतेषु सन्तुष्ट्या येन केन वा ।सर्वेन्द्रियोपशान्त्या च तुष्यत्याशु जनार्दन: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dayayā sarva-bhūteṣusantuṣṭyā yena kena vāsarvendriyopaśāntyā catuṣyaty āśu janārdanaḥ
verses.text: By showing mercy to all living entities, being satisfied somehow or other, and restricting the senses from sense enjoyment, one can very quickly satisfy the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Janārdana.

verses.sanskrit: दयया सर्वभूतेषु सन्तुष्ट्या येन केन वा ।सर्वेन्द्रियोपशान्त्या च तुष्यत्याशु जनार्दन: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dayayā sarva-bhūteṣusantuṣṭyā yena kena vāsarvendriyopaśāntyā catuṣyaty āśu janārdanaḥ
verses.text: By showing mercy to all living entities, being satisfied somehow or other, and restricting the senses from sense enjoyment, one can very quickly satisfy the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Janārdana.

verses.sanskrit: अपहतसकलैषणामलात्म-न्यविरतमेधितभावनोपहूत: ।निजजनवशगत्वमात्मनोऽय-न्न सरति छिद्रवदक्षर: सतां हि ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: apahata-sakalaiṣaṇāmalātmanyaviratam edhita-bhāvanopahūtaḥnija-jana-vaśa-gatvam ātmano ’yanna sarati chidravad akṣaraḥ satāṁ hi
verses.text: Being completely cleansed of all material desires, the devotees are freed from all mental contamination. Thus they can always think of the Lord constantly and address Him very feelingly. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, knowing Himself to be controlled by His devotees, does not leave them for a second, just as the sky overhead never becomes invisible.

verses.sanskrit: अपहतसकलैषणामलात्म-न्यविरतमेधितभावनोपहूत: ।निजजनवशगत्वमात्मनोऽय-न्न सरति छिद्रवदक्षर: सतां हि ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: apahata-sakalaiṣaṇāmalātmanyaviratam edhita-bhāvanopahūtaḥnija-jana-vaśa-gatvam ātmano ’yanna sarati chidravad akṣaraḥ satāṁ hi
verses.text: Being completely cleansed of all material desires, the devotees are freed from all mental contamination. Thus they can always think of the Lord constantly and address Him very feelingly. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, knowing Himself to be controlled by His devotees, does not leave them for a second, just as the sky overhead never becomes invisible.

verses.sanskrit: अपहतसकलैषणामलात्म-न्यविरतमेधितभावनोपहूत: ।निजजनवशगत्वमात्मनोऽय-न्न सरति छिद्रवदक्षर: सतां हि ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: apahata-sakalaiṣaṇāmalātmanyaviratam edhita-bhāvanopahūtaḥnija-jana-vaśa-gatvam ātmano ’yanna sarati chidravad akṣaraḥ satāṁ hi
verses.text: Being completely cleansed of all material desires, the devotees are freed from all mental contamination. Thus they can always think of the Lord constantly and address Him very feelingly. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, knowing Himself to be controlled by His devotees, does not leave them for a second, just as the sky overhead never becomes invisible.

verses.sanskrit: न भजति कुमनीषिणां स इज्यांहरिरधनात्मधनप्रियो रसज्ञ: ।श्रुतधनकुलकर्मणां मदैर्येविदधति पापमकिञ्चनेषु सत्सु ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: na bhajati kumanīṣiṇāṁ sa ijyāṁharir adhanātma-dhana-priyo rasa-jñaḥśruta-dhana-kula-karmaṇāṁ madair yevidadhati pāpam akiñcaneṣu satsu
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead becomes very dear to those devotees who have no material possessions but are fully happy in possessing the devotional service of the Lord. Indeed, the Lord relishes the devotional activities of such devotees. Those who are puffed up with material education, wealth, aristocracy and fruitive activity are very proud of possessing material things, and they often deride the devotees. Even if such people offer the Lord worship, the Lord never accepts them.

verses.sanskrit: श्रियमनुचरतीं तदर्थिनश्चद्विपदपतीन् विबुधांश्च यत्स्वपूर्ण: ।न भजति निजभृत्यवर्गतन्त्र:कथममुमुद्विसृजेत्पुमान् कृतज्ञ: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śriyam anucaratīṁ tad-arthinaś cadvipada-patīn vibudhāṁś ca yat sva-pūrṇaḥna bhajati nija-bhṛtya-varga-tantraḥkatham amum udvisṛjet pumān kṛta-jñaḥ
verses.text: Although the Supreme Personality of Godhead is self-sufficient, He becomes dependent on His devotees. He does not care for the goddess of fortune, nor for the kings and demigods who are after the favors of the goddess of fortune. Where is that person who is actually grateful and will not worship the Personality of Godhead?

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचइति प्रचेतसो राजन्नन्याश्च भगवत्कथा: ।श्रावयित्वा ब्रह्मलोकं ययौ स्वायम्भुवो मुनि: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaiti pracetaso rājannanyāś ca bhagavat-kathāḥśrāvayitvā brahma-lokaṁyayau svāyambhuvo muniḥ
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: My dear King Vidura, Śrī Nārada Muni, the son of Lord Brahmā, thus described all these relationships with the Supreme Personality of Godhead to the Pracetās. Afterward, he returned to Brahmaloka.

verses.sanskrit: तेऽपि तन्मुखनिर्यातं यशो लोकमलापहम् ।हरेर्निशम्य तत्पादं ध्यायन्तस्तद्गतिं ययु: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: te ’pi tan-mukha-niryātaṁyaśo loka-malāpahamharer niśamya tat-pādaṁdhyāyantas tad-gatiṁ yayuḥ
verses.text: Hearing from Nārada’s mouth the glories of the Lord, which vanquish all the ill fortune of the world, the Pracetās also became attached to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Meditating on His lotus feet, they advanced to the ultimate destination.

verses.sanskrit: तेऽपि तन्मुखनिर्यातं यशो लोकमलापहम् ।हरेर्निशम्य तत्पादं ध्यायन्तस्तद्गतिं ययु: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: te ’pi tan-mukha-niryātaṁyaśo loka-malāpahamharer niśamya tat-pādaṁdhyāyantas tad-gatiṁ yayuḥ
verses.text: Hearing from Nārada’s mouth the glories of the Lord, which vanquish all the ill fortune of the world, the Pracetās also became attached to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Meditating on His lotus feet, they advanced to the ultimate destination.

verses.sanskrit: एतत्तेऽभिहितं क्षत्तर्यन्मां त्वं परिपृष्टवान् ।प्रचेतसां नारदस्य संवादं हरिकीर्तनम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: etat te ’bhihitaṁ kṣattaryan māṁ tvaṁ paripṛṣṭavānpracetasāṁ nāradasyasaṁvādaṁ hari-kīrtanam
verses.text: My dear Vidura, I have told you everything you wanted to know about the conversation between Nārada and the Pracetās, the conversation describing the glories of the Lord. I have related this as far as possible.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचय एष उत्तानपदो मानवस्यानुवर्णित: ।वंश: प्रियव्रतस्यापि निबोध नृपसत्तम ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaya eṣa uttānapadomānavasyānuvarṇitaḥvaṁśaḥ priyavratasyāpinibodha nṛpa-sattama
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: O best of kings [King Parīkṣit], I have now finished telling about the descendants of the first son of Svāyambhuva Manu, Uttānapāda. I shall now try to relate the activities of the descendants of Priyavrata, the second son of Svāyambhuva Manu. Please hear them attentively.

verses.sanskrit: इमां तु कौषारविणोपवर्णितांक्षत्ता निशम्याजितवादसत्कथाम् ।प्रवृद्धभावोऽश्रुकलाकुलो मुने-र्दधार मूर्ध्ना चरणं हृदा हरे: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: imāṁ tu kauṣāraviṇopavarṇitāṁkṣattā niśamyājita-vāda-sat-kathāmpravṛddha-bhāvo ’śru-kalākulo munerdadhāra mūrdhnā caraṇaṁ hṛdā hareḥ
verses.text: My dear King, in this way, after hearing the transcendental messages of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and His devotees from the great sage Maitreya, Vidura was overwhelmed with ecstasy. With tears in his eyes, he immediately fell down at the lotus feet of his guru, his spiritual master. He then fixed the Supreme Personality of Godhead within the core of his heart.

verses.sanskrit: इमां तु कौषारविणोपवर्णितांक्षत्ता निशम्याजितवादसत्कथाम् ।प्रवृद्धभावोऽश्रुकलाकुलो मुने-र्दधार मूर्ध्ना चरणं हृदा हरे: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: imāṁ tu kauṣāraviṇopavarṇitāṁkṣattā niśamyājita-vāda-sat-kathāmpravṛddha-bhāvo ’śru-kalākulo munerdadhāra mūrdhnā caraṇaṁ hṛdā hareḥ
verses.text: My dear King, in this way, after hearing the transcendental messages of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and His devotees from the great sage Maitreya, Vidura was overwhelmed with ecstasy. With tears in his eyes, he immediately fell down at the lotus feet of his guru, his spiritual master. He then fixed the Supreme Personality of Godhead within the core of his heart.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचसोऽयमद्य महायोगिन् भवता करुणात्मना ।दर्शितस्तमस: पारो यत्राकिञ्चनगो हरि: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācaso ’yam adya mahā-yoginbhavatā karuṇātmanādarśitas tamasaḥ pāroyatrākiñcana-go hariḥ
verses.text: Śrī Vidura said: O great mystic, O greatest of all devotees, by your causeless mercy I have been shown the path of liberation from this world of darkness. By following this path, a person liberated from the material world can return home, back to Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचइत्यानम्य तमामन्‍त्र्य विदुरो गजसाह्वयम् ।स्वानां दिद‍ृक्षु: प्रययौ ज्ञातीनां निर्वृताशय: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaity ānamya tam āmantryaviduro gajasāhvayamsvānāṁ didṛkṣuḥ prayayaujñātīnāṁ nirvṛtāśayaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: Vidura thus offered obeisances unto the great sage Maitreya and, taking his permission, started for the city of Hastināpura to see his own kinsmen, although he had no material desires.
